steps.title: Locate the clue about the historical norm
steps.content: The critical clue is “highly decorated and detailed sets were not common until the 1600s.” This establishes that in the distant past, simple stages were the standard practice.

steps.title: Determine what audiences would experience as standard
steps.content:
The blank needs a phrase describing how ancient audiences related to simple stages — since such stages were the norm before 1600, audiences would have been used to them. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — disliked by: implies ancient audiences had a negative reaction — but the passage gives no reason to think they objected; simple stages were simply the standard. ✗
  • Choice B — confusing to: implies something unfamiliar or puzzling — but simple stages were the norm, so they would cause no confusion at all. ✗
  • Choice C — expected by: implies the norm that audiences were accustomed to — fits exactly; since elaborate sets were not yet common, plain stages were what audiences anticipated. ✓
  • Choice D — exciting to: implies novelty or thrill — but something routine is not exciting; the passage contrasts simple stages as the past standard against today’s immersive sets. ✗
C is correct.
